subject: xone: update to latest.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: No effect — xone Xbox wireless adapter driver is now sourced from Buildroot package (dlundqvist/xone) instead of fork's vendored drivers/hid/xone/

license_provenance: GPL-2.0-or-later (vendored from medusalix/xone; dlundqvist fork inherits same license)
notes: GROUPED ROW in provenance.md §3.5: all 7 xone commits (4ddd8ec3d, 5a7965488, c708f2222, e2eb39e6f, 8270e78f4, d776ddb4e, d5beb5aa6) listed in one row — escalation trigger. This commit (c708f2222 'xone: update to latest') is a pure refactoring of the vendored driver (code quality improvements to rumble handling, initialization order). Commit is now dead code in fork: xone has been re-sourced as a Buildroot out-of-tree kernel-module package (package/xone), pinned to dlundqvist/xone (f2aa9fe...) as of 2026-07-13. No kernel patches carry xone; no Kconfig option exists in our board/mister/de10nano/linux.config. Dropping this commit from the fork has no user-visible effect: the xone driver still ships in the image via the Buildroot package. The package's comment (xone.mk:41-49) verifies that all 4 of stock's local deltas (rumble fix, Elite-2 paddle backport, per-PID firmware naming, sysfs pairing attribute) are already native features of dlundqvist/xone — no compat patches needed. This commit is equivalent to dlundqvist/xone's code maturity at that point (2022-04-13 was near initial fork time); the package is now ahead. sonnet-verified: package supersession claim re-confirmed directly against package/xone/xone.mk (local checkout) — XONE_VERSION=f2aa9fe01103d7600553b505b298ff0bd47ff280 pinned from dlundqvist/xone, and the 'LOCAL DELTAS' comment block (xone.mk:42-49, off by one from this record's cited :41-49) does state all four of stock's local deltas (rumble handling, Elite-2 paddles, per-PID firmware, sysfs pairing) are already native upstream features. Disposition=dropped-deliberate and severity=feature-loss (relative to a hypothetical drop with no replacement — mitigated to none in practice since package/xone covers it) confirmed correct. NOTE: sibling record 5a7965488 (xone rumble one-liner) claims a specific 'gip_gamepad_send_rumble line 167' citation for the rumble-handling claim referenced here — that specific citation was found to be WRONG on independent verification (see that record); the general 'rumble handling exists' claim in xone.mk itself is not disputed, only the specific line/function citation in the sibling record.
